# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.
# Category: container_security

# DockerFile setup with a base image of Ubuntu that includes some packages required by user-requested tasks 
FROM ubuntu:latest   # Use latest version if you need, or use specific tagged versions e.g.,Ubuntu18.04 LTS (available in the docker hub) for security and convenience reasons but it's best to stick with current stable image when possible so as not overcomplicate your Docker setup
SHELL ["bash", "-c"]  # Use bash shell, which is preferred instead of sh or dash if you have a large number of dependencies. Also using '-c' argument in docker file allows us execute commands directly into the running container to avoid building and then run it everytime we use this Dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && \    # Updating packages lists for installing other necessary package (like curl, wget etc) 
apt-get install -yq --no-install-recommends curl unzip git nodejs; rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*   # Installing required utilities and remove the list of cached files to free up space in Ubuntu. If you use different base image, update it as needed
COPY . ./    # Copy all code from our current directory into container's root(/) 
WORKDIR /uploads     # Set working dir for subsequent copy operations inside Dockerfile (this will be the location where uploaded files are to go), after installation in Ubuntu they end up being under '/usr/share/nginx/html')
RUN chmod -R a+rwx ./    # Setting read, write and execute permissions so our server can access these directories.  This line is specific for this task requirement because uploads directory should be writable by the user running Docker commands to avoid any issues related permission errors when copying files later on downstream in stream processing step
VOLUME [/uploads]    # Creating a volume that will persist outside of container life cycle, named 'vol_1'.  It can still access these data if needed by other containers. Also it makes the directory /usr/share/nginx html available to all users inside Docker with command: ls -la
CMD ["node", "app"]   # The CMD instruction allows a container to run as though it was just started from this image and invoked directly, so you can include commands once where they're needed. In our case app is the default file runner in npm which runs whatever command given via ENV var 'npm start'.
